objects.name: Analytic 1830
objects.description: The defender correlates creation of background scheduler activity with later execution of repeating or deferred work by the same managed app, then raises confidence when the triggered activity produces network, local-write, or other app behavior that occurs outside expected user context. Because iOS exposes weaker direct scheduling observability in many enterprise environments, the analytic anchors first on managed app posture and lifecycle-to-network or lifecycle-to-file effects, with NSBackgroundActivityScheduler-related behavior treated as strongest when runtime telemetry can observe background scheduler usage or execution callbacks.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobiledEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Scheduled task execution creates cache, staged payload, local output, or collected data artifact immediately after wake or job trigger

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: Application creates or executes NSBackgroundActivityScheduler activity with repeating or deferred invocation semantics during the scheduling and trigger phases

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between scheduler creation, later execution, and follow-on file or network behavior
Managed apps legitimately expected to perform background maintenance or deferred sync behavior
Expected repeating interval or defer window for legitimate background activity
Whether follow-on behavior from background scheduler execution should require recent user interaction
Maximum expected delay between scheduled execution and outbound communication
Minimum outbound volume after scheduled execution to treat network behavior as meaningful
